questions.text: Delivery block when applied, blocks the sales order for delivery.
Order the steps required to remove blocking rules: - Remove User Status at Header - Line-item User Status - Rejection reason - Remove Delivery Block
options: Line-item User Status → Rejection reason → Remove Delivery Block → Remove User Status at Header
Rejection reason → Remove Delivery Block → Line-item User Status → Remove User Status at Header
Remove User Status at Header → Remove Delivery Block → Line-item User Status → Rejection reason
Remove Delivery Block → Line-item User Status → Rejection reason → Remove User Status at Header

questions.text: An active header user status does not prevent delivery from being created for the entire order
Item rejected  are considered in the general net value of the sales order,  ATP confirmed stock will be removed, and the line will not be delivered/shipped
Entity decides to activate or not each blocking for the Sales Area + Sales Order Type
Incompletion  is managed manually as long as  there is a missing information in the sales order
Why do we have sales order blocks?
options: A control to check data accuracy on the sales order
To ensure the sales order is being processed correctly to our customer’s expectations
Improve our business processes by measuring the block instances on our Sales Orders
Highlight and communicate any process change needed with our customers

questions.text: According to Control LoC1, what triggers a mandatory validation workflow for free goods orders?
options: All free goods orders, regardless of value. B) Free goods orders placed using specific dedicated order types. C) Free goods orders exceeding locally defined thresholdsD) Free goods orders that are flagged as potentially high-risk.
